text: Gut dysbiosis features one or more of the following non-mutually exclusive characteristics 1,2:      Bloom of pathobionts (members of the commensal microbiota that have the potential to cause pathology). Such bacteria are typically present at low relative abundances but proliferate when aberrations occur in the intestinal ecosystem      Loss of commensals, the reduction or complete loss of normally residing members of the microbiota, which can be the consequence of microbial killing or diminished bacterial proliferation      Loss of diversity, characteristic of disease-associated dysbiosis
